stories.title: LeBron James Day 5 — Down to Warriors vs. Cavs in 50/50 Battle

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
Per insiders, the LeBron sweepstakes is officially 50/50.

Warriors or Cavaliers. Bay Area or back home.

Rich Paul's bombshell: "If the Knicks hadn't won, he'd be going to New York."

48 hours until July 6 decides everything. 👑

#NBA #NBAFreeAgency

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Day 5 of LeBron watch and the answer is still Warriors or Cavs.

One insider says it's "exactly 50/50."

Moratorium ends July 6 at 12:01 PM ET.

Warriors or Cavaliers? Drop your pick. 👇

#NBAFreeAgency

facebook.long_form:
👑 THE LEBRON DECISION — DAY 5 UPDATE

We are 48 hours away from the NBA moratorium ending on July 6, and LeBron James' next team is still unknown — but the picture is getting clearer.

Per Shams Charania, James is deciding between six franchises: the Golden State Warriors, Cleveland Cavaliers, Miami Heat, Denver Nuggets, Philadelphia 76ers, and Minnesota Timberwolves. But multiple league insiders have confirmed it has effectively come down to two. A Warriors insider told reporters: "It's about 50/50 right now. It's Golden State and Cleveland."

The Warriors pitch: A final championship run alongside Stephen Curry, LeBron's partner from the 2024 Paris Olympics gold medal team. The Bay Area faithful. One more ring before the curtain falls.

The Cavaliers pitch: Home. Akron, Ohio. 2016. The city LeBron already saved once — can he do it again at 41 years old? Cleveland can also offer more money as a team LeBron previously played for.

And then there's the bombshell from agent Rich Paul: "If the Knicks hadn't won, there would be no board. He'd be going to the Knicks." The Knicks won the 2026 championship. That door closed. Now the board has two names on it: Warriors and Cavaliers.

The answer comes July 6. Which side are you on? Drop a 🏀 for Warriors or a 🔴 for Cavaliers in the comments.
facebook.image_caption: LeBron's decision is 48 hours away. Warriors or Cavs? The NBA world is holding its breath. 👑 Drop your pick below. #NBA #LeBron

article.headline: LeBron James Day 5: Warriors or Cavaliers? The NBA's Biggest Decision Comes Down to 50/50

stories.title: Two Days Out — The July 6 Trade Execution Preview

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
48 hours until July 6 reshapes the NBA forever.

🔥 Giannis officially a Miami Heat
🍁 Kawhi officially back in Toronto
🔔 Brown officially in Philadelphia
💚 George officially a Celtic

All official Monday 12:01 PM ET. #NBA

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
The July 6 mega-trades become OFFICIAL in less than 36 hours.

Giannis to Miami. Kawhi to Toronto. Brown to Philly.

The NBA you know is about to look completely different.

Are you ready? 🔥

#NBA #NBAFreeAgency

facebook.long_form:
🗓️ JULY 6 IS ALMOST HERE — The Day the NBA Changes Forever

Monday, July 6 at 12:01 PM ET, the moratorium ends and the NBA's biggest offseason in a decade becomes official. Here's what executes:

🔥 GIANNIS TO MIAMI — The Greek Freak officially becomes a Miami Heat player, joining Bam Adebayo in one of the most dominant frontcourts in NBA history. Milwaukee gets Tyler Herro, Kel'el Ware, Jaime Jaquez Jr., Kasparas Jakučionis, the No. 13 pick, three first-round picks, and a swap right.

🍁 KAWHI BACK IN TORONTO — The Klaw returns to the franchise where he had his signature moment in 2019, his "Shot Heard Round the World" that led to the Raptors' championship. Kawhi Leonard gets to close out his career where he was a champion. The Clippers receive Brandon Ingram and future picks in return.

🔔 BROWN TO PHILLY — Jaylen Brown officially joins Joel Embiid and Tyrese Maxey in Philadelphia. Paul George heads to Boston to pair with Jayson Tatum.

All of this in a single hour Monday afternoon. The NBA's power map is being completely redrawn. Which team do you think benefits most from July 6? Tell us in the comments.
facebook.image_caption: 48 hours. Giannis to Miami. Kawhi to Toronto. Brown to Philly. July 6 at 12:01 PM ET — the NBA you know changes forever. 🔥 #NBA

article.headline: July 6 Mega-Trade Day: The NBA's Most Historic Hour Is 48 Hours Away

stories.title: Lakers Continue Retool — Ayton to Wizards, Sexton and Grimes Signed

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Post-LeBron Lakers moving FAST:

❌ Ayton traded to Washington
✅ Walker Kessler signed (4yr/$130M)
✅ Collin Sexton signed
✅ Quentin Grimes signed

They're not rebuilding. They're retooling. 🟣🟡

#Lakers #NBA

facebook.long_form:
🟡💜 THE POST-LEBRON LAKERS ARE MOVING FAST

LeBron is gone — and the Los Angeles Lakers are not standing still.

In the last 24 hours, the Lakers have traded Deandre Ayton to the Washington Wizards (receiving Jaden Hardy and two future second-round picks in 2031 and 2032), signed guard Collin Sexton, signed wing Quentin Grimes, and added center Sandro Mamukelashvili.

This comes after already landing Walker Kessler from Utah on a 4-year, $130 million deal — the defensive anchor the rebuild needs.

What is taking shape in Los Angeles: a Kessler-anchored defense with Sexton and Grimes providing perimeter energy and scoring punch. It is not a championship roster yet — but it is a foundation with young, mobile pieces around a legitimate defensive anchor.

For the Wizards, Ayton gives them a veteran big man alongside No. 1 overall pick AJ Dybantsa, who just signed his 4-year, $66.91 million rookie deal. Washington is building young from multiple directions.

The Lakers are trusting the process. Do you believe in it? Share your thoughts below.
facebook.image_caption: Ayton out. Kessler in. Sexton and Grimes signed. The post-LeBron Lakers are already building. 💜💛 #Lakers #NBA

article.headline: Post-LeBron Lakers Move Fast: Ayton to Washington, Sexton and Grimes Signed

stories.title: Celtics Pivot After Brown Trade — Mitchell Robinson Signed

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
Celtics add Mitchell Robinson on a 3yr/$47M deal.

They lost Jaylen Brown. They gain:
→ Paul George (3x All-Star)
→ Mitchell Robinson (elite rim protector)
→ Multiple future 1st-round picks

Don't sleep on Boston. ☘️ #Celtics #NBA

facebook.long_form:
☘️ CELTICS PIVOT UNDERWAY — Mitchell Robinson Signed 3yr/$47M

Boston traded Jaylen Brown to Philadelphia for Paul George and a haul of draft picks. At first glance, it looked like a disaster for Celtics fans. But look closer — Boston is rapidly filling the gaps.

Mitchell Robinson is the newest Celtic, signing a 3-year, $47 million contract. Robinson is one of the elite rim protectors in the league — a low-post anchor who guards the paint and cleans the glass with efficiency. In a system built around Jayson Tatum and Paul George, Robinson's role is clear: protect the rim, control the boards, stay out of foul trouble.

Boston also added veteran point guard Mike Conley on a 1-year deal.

The new-look Celtics core: Jayson Tatum. Paul George. Mitchell Robinson. Mike Conley. The Celtics lost a Finals MVP but they added a three-time All-Star, an elite rim protector, and substantial draft capital for the future. The Brad Stevens rebuild plan is in effect.

Are the Celtics actually in a stronger position than people think? Comment below.
facebook.image_caption: Mitchell Robinson 3yr/$47M to Boston. The Celtics are building around Tatum and George. Don't write them off. ☘️ #Celtics #NBA

article.headline: Celtics Sign Mitchell Robinson: Boston's Post-Brown Pivot Is Taking Shape

stories.title: Summer League Tips Off — Rookies Hit the Court on July 4

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Independence Day basketball is here 🎆

The NBA Summer League tips off in Salt Lake City:
🏀 Grizzlies vs. Thunder — 3 PM ET
🏀 Hawks vs. Jazz — 5 PM ET

The next generation takes the court. Happy 4th! 🇺🇸

#NBA #SummerLeague

facebook.long_form:
🎆 HAPPY JULY 4TH — AND THE NBA SUMMER LEAGUE IS HERE!

While fireworks light up the sky, basketball is being played. The 2026 NBA Salt Lake City Summer League tipped off today at the Jon M. Huntsman Center at the University of Utah, featuring four teams: the Utah Jazz, Atlanta Hawks, Memphis Grizzlies, and Oklahoma City Thunder.

Today's games: Grizzlies vs. Thunder at 3 PM ET and Hawks vs. Jazz at 5 PM ET.

The Summer League is where NBA futures are on display. Draft picks get their first taste of professional competition. Undrafted free agents fight for roster spots that could define their careers. The California Classic — in Sacramento and San Francisco — also tipped off July 3.

The big one: Las Vegas Summer League begins July 9 and runs through July 19, featuring all 30 teams. That is where No. 1 overall pick AJ Dybantsa of the Washington Wizards will get his highest-profile moments before the regular season begins.

The next wave of NBA stars is taking their first steps. Which rookie are you most excited to watch? Drop their name in the comments!
facebook.image_caption: The Summer League is live on July 4th! 🎆🏀 Next-gen NBA stars take the court. Which rookie are you watching? #NBA #SummerLeague
